rights_policy: Link-only factual indexing. Do not vendor guide text, photos, video, maps, or social-media content without item-level permission.

stationing_policy.published: Preserve a source-published river mile or kilometer and convert it to meters from the authored put-in.
stationing_policy.ordered_only: Generate a visibly provisional station from published downstream order so the editor can display a marker; replace it with GPS, imagery, or guide-reviewed stationing before terrain or solver promotion.
stationing_policy.no_geometry_claim: A marker records review intent and does not assert exact bank, bed, hazard, or runnable-line geometry.

South Fork American River Map
Behind the Scenery: Colorado River Rapids

sources.evidence_scope: Bidwell Rapid class, scout recognition, and low/medium/high 08MA002 flow-photo context; no copied media or exact geometry.

sources.title: Rafting Chilko River, North America's Most Notorious Rapids
sources.evidence_scope: Published downstream-name evidence for Bidwell, Lava Canyon, White Mile, Green Mile, and Miracle Canyon; no exact stations.

sources.title: American Whitewater Accident Report 86686: Chilko White Mile
sources.evidence_scope: White Mile high-flow flip, midstream-rock, cold-water, and long-swim consequence evidence; historical report is not an exact line or station source.

sources.title: American Whitewater Reach 148: South Fork American, Chili Bar Dam to Coloma
sources.evidence_scope: Meat Grinder right-side standard line, hidden holes, railroad-grade scout/portage trail, and foot-entrapment/flush-drowning history; Troublemaker rocky-island split, Gunsight final drop, Double Trouble alternate right line, and downstream Hazard Rock pourover with foot-entrapment crack. Also: Chili Bar Hole play hole with river-left eddy service, Racehorse Bend's sharp left turn toward a right-side rock wall, Maya's surf wave with large eddies on both sides, Rock Garden's small boulders, African Queen's center-right line through vegetated outcrops, First/Second/Third Threat holes and eddies, and the 3,500-7,000 cfs high-flow difficulty rise.

sources.title: GoRafting South Fork American Rapid Guide: Meat Grinder
sources.evidence_scope: Right-of-center entry, two offset mid-river holes split left-to-right, Death Star Rock rooster-tail flat-wrap hazard, exit wave train with larger middle wave, and flow thresholds (~2,000 cfs wave/lateral transition, ~5,000 cfs Son of Meat Grinder laterals).

sources.title: GoRafting South Fork American Rapid Guide: Troublemaker
sources.evidence_scope: Left-bank scout eddy, tree-island right-channel entry, lead-in and runout center sieve piles, S-bend right lateral, clip-left main-hole move, Peanut Gallery left wall, Snaggle Tooth pyramid rock, right safety eddy, and high-water (~3,500+ cfs) left-of-island and Double Trouble alternatives.

sources.title: American River Resort: Troublemaker!
sources.evidence_scope: Boulder garden into a falls with far-left S channel, low-flow narrow path, right diagonal wave flip hazard, hard-exit left eddy, gut-of-the-hole placement, Gunsight Rock wrap channel, and the advanced eddy-left ferry exit.

sources.title: OARS: The 10 Best Whitewater Rapids on the American River
sources.evidence_scope: Troublemaker zig-zags boater's left to right; the main drop is split in two by Gunsight Rock; rafts bounce off either side of Gunsight or occasionally run directly over the top. Also: Triple Threat as a three-part rapid with big holes on the first and third sections and a flip on the first hit that can keep swimmers in until after the third; Satan's Cesspool's final big drop and standing wave; Bouncing Rock rafts bouncing off the boulder and slinging into the big hole; Hospital Bar's single big wave and Gold Rush hospital-tent name.

sources.title: Whitewater Guidebook: South Fork of the American River
sources.evidence_scope: Meatgrinder as a long, tricky rapid with big hydraulics; Troublemaker as the most difficult rapid on the South Fork, scouted from river left. Also: Maya's kayak surf wave at most flows, First Threat's nearly river-wide surf hole, Fowler's Rock as the first class III below Troublemaker, Satan's Cesspool as the biggest rapid of the Gorge run, Weber Creek entering on river left during Bouncing Rock, and Hospital Bar as the last big ride.

sources.title: River Brain: Meatgrinder (Quarter Mile) Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: Quarter Mile alias, class III-III+ range, long continuous rocky character with most water funneling toward the bottom near the right bank, and an 800-4,000 cfs recommended flow range.

sources.title: River Brain: Troublemaker (S-Turn) Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: S-Turn alias, class III-IV range, two stacked holes (one at the start, one immediately below on the sharp right-hand turn), the squeeze between Gunsight Rock and the left bank, left-bank scouting, and holes growing bigger and more powerful as levels rise.

sources.title: Garden Betty: Troublemaker on the American River (trip report)
sources.evidence_scope: First-person run at 1,600 cfs: powerful diagonal wave, swift-moving hole, big drop next to Gunsight Rock, kayak eddy-left ferry line past Gunsight, and the head-on drop line between two boulders.

sources.title: GoRafting South Fork American River Rafting Guide + Map
sources.evidence_scope: Inline rapid-by-rapid notes: Chili Bar Surf Wave left eddy and ~1,700 cfs crash threshold; Racehorse Bend right-wall lateral at the bend apex; Maya middle line, right sneak, and ~3,000 cfs holes; Rock Garden weave; African Queen right-entry cut-left line and bottom wave; Frog Rock wrap hazard and the First/Second/Third Threat lines; Fowler's runout toward Son of Fowler's two holes above ~3,000 cfs; Upper Haystack head-on waves into a pool; Lost Hat lead-in to Satan's Cesspool; Son of Satan's right-of-rock then work-left move; Scissors left-of-boulder finish; Lower Haystack mid-river rock and 4,500+ cfs crashing wave; Bouncing Rock apex hole; Pre-Op right-of-center line and high-flow hole; Hospital Bar lateral, Catchers Mitt, and photo spot; Recovery Room Little Big Hole and reservoir-stage behavior; Surprise pourover rock with left avoidance.

sources.title: GoRafting South Fork American Rapid Guide: Fowler's Rock
sources.evidence_scope: House Rock thread-the-needle left entry, the start-left-then-ferry-right alternate, middle-channel and left-side holes, the right-of-Fowler's-Rock runout, and the left-of-Fowler's-Rock line that opens above ~2,500 cfs and becomes the line of choice as flows rise.

sources.title: GoRafting South Fork American Rapid Guide: Satan's Cesspool
sources.evidence_scope: Right-shelf constriction with a substantial lateral to T up, a second straightforward wave and swirly runout, ~4,000 cfs left-wall push turning flippy, 6,000+ cfs island submergence with a tumultuous right eddy line below, the photo-rapid role, and the backboard tied to the left shore below.

sources.title: American Whitewater Reach 4068: South Fork American, The Gorge (Greenwood Creek to Folsom)
sources.evidence_scope: Satan's Cesspool island and left-cliff dogleg with twin ledges and a swimmer catch pool, the ~70-yard spacing to Son of Satan's and their high-flow merge, Son of Satan's right-wall push with an easy move left, Bouncing Rock's far-right drop-and-hole with a swept-in warning, Hospital Bar's chute carrying the river's largest wave, Fowler's Rock giant-rock slalom hydraulics, Surprise's reservoir-dependent wave/hole avoidable on either side, photographer positions at Satan's Cesspool, and 1,200-1,500 cfs typical summer flows.

sources.title: River Brain: American River, South Fork - Chili Bar run
sources.evidence_scope: Per-rapid class ranges for the Chili Bar run, Rock Garden's rock outcrops lining the river, First Threat's sharp right turn into a sizable hole, Second Threat's wave train toward river right, and Third Threat as a wave-train drop.

sources.title: River Brain: American River, South Fork - The Gorge run
sources.evidence_scope: Gorge per-rapid class ranges and aliases (Son of Satan's as Dead Man's Drop; Pre-Op as Triage), Fowler's zig-zag past very large rocks, Lost Hat leading into Satan's Cesspool, Satan's Cesspool as the gorge crux with a large hole where the river cuts through bedrock, Son of Satan's current piling on the right bank, Bouncing Rock's right hole with left sneak options, Hospital Bar's left-facing diagonal hole growing bigger and more retentive to the right, Recovery Room's punch-through hole, and Surprise's low-reservoir funnel constriction.

sources.title: River Brain: Chili Bar Hole Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: Class III- to III range, a fairly retentive but playable hole just below the put-in, left-eddy access, commercial rafts surfing it for splash, and an 800-4,000 cfs recommended flow range.

sources.title: River Brain: Racehorse Bend (Sluice Box) Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: Sluice Box alias, class III- to III+ range, follow-the-main-wave-train-left guidance, exit current pushing toward the right bank, numerous lateral waves that flip inflatable and rigid kayaks, and an 800-4,000 cfs recommended flow range.

sources.title: River Brain: Maya (Traffic Light) Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: Traffic Light alias, class II+ to III range, class II waves leading into a river-right drop at the bottom, a left drop opening as flows rise, a surfable main hole at the bottom, eddy access on both sides, and an 800-4,000 cfs recommended flow range.

sources.title: River Brain: African Queen Rapid, South Fork American Chili Bar
sources.evidence_scope: Class III to IV range (an outlier against the class II published elsewhere), several small islands separating the river, most water down the right channel as the raft line, kayaks exploring the other channels, and an 800-4,000 cfs recommended flow range.

rivers.display_name: South Fork American: Chili Bar to Folsom Reservoir

class_reported_range.note: AW reach 148 lists class II; the AW map II+; GoRafting rates the surf wave III; River Brain reports III- to III.

relative_position.notes: Small rapid under the Chili Bar bridge just below the put-in; a fairly retentive but playable hole (River Brain) that commercial rafts deliberately surf for splash.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: excellent play at release flows (AW reach 148); the hole is well formed and friendly at release levels.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: prime raft-surf window - the AW map calls the surfing fun around ~1,500 cfs, just below GoRafting's ~1,700 cfs threshold where the wave begins to crash.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: above ~1,700 cfs the wave begins to crash a bit (GoRafting); the feature is pushier and less of a controlled surf.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Fairly retentive but playable (River Brain); the standard warm-up surf for commercial trips.

relative_position.notes: Eddy service on river left (AW reach 148); a large eddy on river left gives repeat access to the surf wave (GoRafting; River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: easy, well-defined eddy; the standard staging point for play sessions.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: still an easy catch supporting lap after lap on the wave (River Brain left-eddy access).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: faster eddy line, but the left side remains the access route.

relative_position.notes: Large pools directly downstream catch swimmers who come out surfing (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: slow, deep pools; easy self-rescue.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the AW map notes large pools below for recovery if paddlers exit the raft.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the pools keep moving; recover swimmers before the rapids downstream.

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates III; the mile guide and American Whitewater reach list III+.

relative_position.notes: Lead-in can be run left or far right at some flows; either way work back to center, entering the main rapid right-of-center.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: rock-dominated; the lead-in channels are shallow and the right-of-center entry line matters most (GoRafting low-flow line, below ~2,000 cfs).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: run the lead-in left or far right and ferry back to center before the main tongue (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: above ~2,000 cfs waves and laterals off the right replace rocks as the primary hazard; the lead-in washes out but pushes harder toward the right-side laterals (GoRafting).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: A swim here is long and rocky: the boulder garden runs over a quarter mile (AW map) and the AW reach history records fatal foot entrapments at summer flows.

relative_position.notes: A small diversion canal removes water on the left; a large rock is visible just left of center and marks the main entry about 10 m to its right (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: rock well exposed and easy to identify; the canal draw on the left is proportionally stronger at low water.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: visible left-of-center landmark for the ~10 m entry offset (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: still visible; the entry read shifts to wave features but the rock remains the entry landmark.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Primarily a navigation landmark; drifting left of it puts the raft into the shallow canal-side rocks.

relative_position.notes: Enter the main rapid about 10 m right of the large rock, slanting left-to-right through a slot between submerged rocks (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the slot is the crux; the submerged rocks are barely covered and the left-to-right slant must be precise.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: an easy move, but failing to make it can have severe consequences because the biggest waves and holes are still ahead (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the slot washes toward waves; missing the move feeds the raft into the right-side laterals with flip potential (GoRafting, above ~2,000 cfs).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Blowing the entry sets the raft up badly for the rest of the quarter-mile boulder garden (AW map).

relative_position.notes: Large boulders and hidden holes over a shallow bed for more than a quarter mile; the standard route stays down the right (AW reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed and most technical; partially exposed or barely covered rocks require continuous maneuvering, and foot-entrapment risk is highest at summer flows (AW reach history).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: rocks barely covered; hidden holes remain the primary hazard (AW reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: rocks cover and the hazard shifts to hydraulics; the AW reach records a flush drowning at high spring flows.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: American Whitewater documents two fatal foot entrapments at summer flows and a flush drowning at high spring flows in this rapid.

relative_position.notes: Two offset holes in the middle of the river; split them moving left to right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the holes read as rocky pourovers; split them on the left-to-right diagonal per the low-flow line (GoRafting, below ~2,000 cfs).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: both offset holes are formed and must be split on the left-to-right diagonal (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: above ~2,000 cfs the holes wash into bigger waves with laterals off the right side; flip potential replaces the split move (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Shallow rock throwing a rooster tail below the offset holes; avoid it by moving right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: barely covered with a prominent rooster tail; the flat-wrap hazard for rafts is at its worst (GoRafting low-flow description).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the rooster tail still marks the rock; move right to clear it (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: deeper cover mutes the rooster tail and the hazard hands off to the right-side laterals (GoRafting, above ~2,000 cfs).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Known to many outfitters as the Death Star Rock; a flat wrap hazard for rafts (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: The wave train and current funnel toward Rhino Rock on the right; most of the water funnels toward the bottom near the right bank (AW map; River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: waves are small and the funnel reads as a rocky channel along the right.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: keep the raft into the waves as the current funnels toward Rhino Rock (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger waves and laterals come off the right side; the large waves have the potential to flip rafts (GoRafting, above ~2,000 cfs).

relative_position.notes: Large rock on the right near the bottom of the rapid where the funneled current converges; wave size decreases below it (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: exposed, with a narrow channel past it.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the current funnels rafts toward it; ride the wave train past on its left (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: partially covered with pushier water driving toward it; contact risk rises with the right-side laterals.

relative_position.notes: After Rhino Rock wave size decreases; eddy out on the left, or finish the rapid along the right (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: well defined and an easy catch.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard regroup eddy after Rhino Rock (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the eddy line is sharper and faster; the catch is harder but it remains the standard regroup.

relative_position.notes: Runout wave train with a larger wave to hit in the middle at the end (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small; mostly rock dodging through the runout.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a fun wave train with a larger middle wave at the end (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: larger; the Son of Meat Grinder rapid below builds right-bank laterals only well above this band (GoRafting notes them above ~5,000 cfs).

relative_position.notes: An excellent trail for scouting and portaging runs on river right along an old railroad grade (AW reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: scout recommended for first-timers; the low-water slot line is easiest to read from the trail.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: full-length right-bank scout and portage option (AW reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: same trail; scouting recommended before committing to the wave-dominated right line.

class_reported_range.note: The AW map and AW reach 148 list III-; the mile guide and GoRafting rate III; River Brain reports III- to III+.

relative_position.notes: An entertaining wave train leads into the left-hand bend (GoRafting); follow the main wave train around to the left (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small waves over a rocky bed; River Brain flags the rapid as rocky at certain levels.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard read-and-run entry; ride the main wave train around to the left (River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger waves with more push toward the right wall at the apex.

relative_position.notes: A large lateral comes off the right wall at the apex of the bend (GoRafting); numerous lateral waves through the rapid can flip inflatable and rigid kayaks (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the lateral is weak, but exposed rock along the right makes the apex tighter.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a fully formed lateral off the right wall (GoRafting); the primary flip hazard for small craft (River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: a stronger lateral with more current driving into the wall at the apex.

relative_position.notes: The river curves left among boulders and runs into an up-tilted slate cliff on the right with submerged rocks along its base (AW map); the current flows sharply left toward the wall (AW reach 148).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the submerged rocks along the cliff are shallowest; contact and pin exposure is highest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the current pushes toward the cliff; paddle hard and precisely (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: strongest push into the wall, and the exit current also piles toward the right bank (River Brain).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Submerged rocks lie along the cliff base (AW map); a raft pushed onto the wall at speed risks a wrap.

relative_position.notes: The safest exit is toward the center (AW map); a closing wave train finishes the rapid (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: thread between exposed boulders to reach the center.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard exit; work off the wall to the center as the bend opens (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the exit current pushes toward the right bank (River Brain); start the move to center early.

relative_position.notes: A large eddy below on the left permits swimming and play (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: an easy, calm catch.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard regroup and swim spot below the bend (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: a faster eddy line, but still the regroup.

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates II; AW reach 148 II+; River Brain II+ to III; the AW map calls it class III above ~3,500 cfs.

relative_position.notes: Class II waves lead into a drop on river right at the bottom (River Brain); the AW map describes a small hole on the right.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a small rocky drop with a modest hit.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the formed right-side drop is the standard fun line - III-ish at certain levels (River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: at GoRafting's ~3,000 cfs threshold large holes and waves develop; the AW map calls Maya class III above ~3,500 cfs with big but easily avoidable holes.

relative_position.notes: A ledge-drop option on the left (AW map); River Brain notes the left drop opens up as flows rise.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: barely runs; the left side is too shallow to be attractive.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: an available alternate to the right-side drop (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the left drop is fully open (River Brain) and the holes grow toward the class III high-water character (AW map).

relative_position.notes: A wonderful surfing wave at many flows (AW reach 148); a great kayak surf wave at most river flows (Whitewater Guidebook); River Brain surfs the main hole at the bottom.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small but well formed; the wave works at many flows (AW reach 148).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: prime playspot, often with long lines of kayakers and a fun wet hit in a raft (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger and crashier; a wet raft hit rather than a controlled surf.

relative_position.notes: Large eddies on both sides serve the play wave; the river-left eddy is particularly huge (AW reach 148); River Brain confirms eddy access on both sides.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: easy catches on either side.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard service eddies for the surf wave (AW reach 148).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: sharper eddy lines, but both sides still stage the playspot.

relative_position.notes: Run down the middle at most water levels and sneak on the right side if needed when high flows build the holes (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: unnecessary; the middle is the line.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the middle line remains standard (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the documented sneak once large holes and waves appear around ~3,000 cfs (GoRafting); the AW map calls the high-water holes easily avoidable.

class_reported_range.note: The AW map, AW reach 148, and GoRafting rate II; River Brain reports II to II+.

relative_position.notes: A maze of small boulders that needs good paddling and boat control (AW map); large rock outcrops line the river for a good stretch (River Brain); weave your way through this minefield (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: at the AW map's documented ~900 cfs threshold; the maze is shallowest and grounding on the small boulders is the main hazard.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: navigable almost anywhere for an enjoyable ride (AW map); an easy rapid with several small boulders (AW reach 148).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: mostly washed; the boulders cover and small boils replace the maze.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Documented coverage for this rapid is thin: an easy class II boulder maze with no named sub-features in any consulted source.

relative_position.notes: At flows under ~900 cfs the rapid is best run far left (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: at the bottom of the review band this is the documented line - under ~900 cfs run far left (AW map).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: optional; most routes through the maze work (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: unnecessary; the maze is covered.

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates II; AW reach 148 and the AW map II+; River Brain publishes an outlier III to IV range - preserved as a source disagreement.

relative_position.notes: Several willow-covered islands split the river into multiple routes (AW map); vegetation-covered rock outcrops (AW reach 148); most flow goes down the right channel (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: only the right channel carries reliable water; the side channels go bony.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: multiple routes are open through the islands (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the islands begin to wash and the channels push harder.

relative_position.notes: The common route runs right almost the entire way, then cuts left at the end (AW map); stay right and cut back left toward the end (GoRafting); the raft line follows most of the water down the right channel (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the only reliable raft route; the cut-left timing matters most at low water.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard center-right line (AW reach 148) with large waves near the second island (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: pushier; the cut-left move comes up faster.

relative_position.notes: A shallow trap at the end of the right channel; cut left to avoid it (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: shallowest; grounding is near-certain if the cut-left move is missed.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: still shallow enough to park a raft; make the documented cut left (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: mostly covered; the cut left remains the clean line.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: A grounding trap rather than a hydraulic; a stuck raft in the current can spin or wrap on the shallows.

relative_position.notes: The most fun route drops over a one-foot falls on the left, then threads a very narrow slot between the islands back to center (AW map); the far-left channel is less interesting.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: below the AW map's ~1,700 cfs raft recommendation; kayak-only at best.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: marginal for rafts - just under the documented ~1,700 cfs threshold (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: open, with the narrow between-island slot pushier.

relative_position.notes: Nice large waves near the second island on the common right route (AW map); a fun wave at the bottom of the rapid (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small splash over shallows.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the fun bottom wave (GoRafting) and large waves near the second island (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: larger waves as the channels merge.

class_reported_range.note: Published collectively as Triple Threat (III); AW reach 148, the AW map, GoRafting, and River Brain index the three parts separately as First Threat (III, River Brain III to III+), Second Threat (II to II+), and Third Threat (II to III).

relative_position.notes: Frog Rock sits in the center of the flow at First Threat and can be passed on either side (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed; the pass on either side is narrowest and wrap risk is highest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: covered enough to hide but still the documented wrap hazard (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: washing over, with strong current still driving onto it.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Known to wrap rafts (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: After a class II lead-in the river turns sharply right and drops into a deep hole along a rock outcropping on the left (AW map; River Brain); nearly river-wide and a favorite kayak surf hole (Whitewater Guidebook).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: smaller but sticky against the outcropping; the boulder-garden lead-in is rockiest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: full size - it buries kayaks and gives raft riders a thrill (AW reach 148); flippiest on the left and mostly avoidable by skirting right (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the biggest hit of the sequence; a flip here means a long swim (OARS).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: OARS: if you flip on the first hit, you might be in the water until after the third - the three Threats run close together.

feature_inventory.display_name: First Threat surf wave and right eddy

relative_position.notes: A punchy standing wave just after the tight right-hand bend is a popular kayak surf spot with eddy access along the right bank (GoRafting); AW reach 148 notes the river-right eddy usually holds a line of waiting kayakers.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small wave, easy eddy; a mellow play session.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: prime surf traffic - expect a queue of kayakers in the right eddy (AW reach 148).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: punchier wave and faster eddy line; play traffic thins.

relative_position.notes: Second Threat drops through a slot along a rock outcropping on the right (AW map); right of center and you're good to go (GoRafting); a wave train toward river right (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the slot is narrow but straightforward to read.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a relatively straightforward rapid to read and run (AW reach 148).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the slot washes into a right-side wave train; AW reach 148 notes the section gets rougher at 3,500-7,000 cfs.

relative_position.notes: A good swimmer's rapid with a large eddy below giving easy access back to the rock outcropping for repeated swims (AW map); BLM toilets sit on river right just below (AW reach 148).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: calm and easy; the standard swim spot.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the documented swimmer's-rapid eddy (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: still catchable, but repeat swims get pushy.

relative_position.notes: A large hole on the far left halfway down Third Threat (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: reads as a rocky pourover on the far left.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a formed hole; avoid it on the center line or run it as the advanced move (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger; OARS counts the third section's hole among the rapid's big hits.

relative_position.notes: A large hole on the far right near the bottom of Third Threat (AW map); running both holes is an advanced maneuver.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: shallow and rocky on the far right.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: formed; the center line avoids it (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger, with more current feeding the far right.

relative_position.notes: The center route through Third Threat is easy and unobstructed (AW map); run right down the middle avoiding the large holes to either side (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: substantial paddling needed to hold the optimal line (AW map).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard line between the flanking holes (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: faster; a swimmer from First Threat can still be in the water through here (OARS).

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates III; the mile guide and AW reach list III+; the AW map and River Brain report up to IV-/IV at flow.

relative_position.notes: Scout from river left; most parties scout (Whitewater Guidebook). Shove off from the scout eddy into the right channel of the tree island (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: scout strongly advised; at lower flows the true path is narrow indeed (AW map).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard river-left scout stop before the drop (Whitewater Guidebook; River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: scout to choose between the standard line and the high-water alternatives that open above ~3,500 cfs (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: The current splits around a rocky island at the lead-in (AW reach); take the right channel of the tree island to follow the deepest flow, then work back left (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the left channel is too shallow; the right channel of the island carries the only reliable water (GoRafting deepest-flow note).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: right channel, then ferry back left to line up the S-bend (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the right channel remains standard; above ~3,500 cfs a left-of-island entry punching two small stacked holes becomes an option (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Just downstream of the tree island is a sieve pile in the center of the flow (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed and most dangerous to a swimmer; stay with the deep right-channel flow.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: covered but straining current; keep rafts and swimmers out of the center below the island (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more water over the pile; still a no-go zone for swimmers.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Sieves strain current through rock; swimmers must avoid the center below the island (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: The rapid zig-zags from boater's left to right (OARS); after the island, enter the S-bend where a small lateral wave off the right side sets up the move onto the main hole (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: at lower flows the true path is narrow indeed (AW map); precise S-turns between exposed rock.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: run at a very fun 1,600 cfs in a June trip report (Garden Betty); clip the main hole on the left with a slight right angle (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the holes get bigger and more powerful as levels rise (River Brain); the S-bend becomes pushier and less forgiving.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Reported class disagrees across sources: III (GoRafting), III+ (mile guide, AW reach), IV- (AW map), III-IV (River Brain).

relative_position.notes: A boulder garden, followed by a falls, with an S channel on the far left (AW map; American River Resort).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: narrow and rocky; the S channel is the technical low-water sneak past the main drop.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: available but tight; most rafts run the main line.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: washes toward the main flow; the documented high-water alternative shifts to the left-of-island stacked-holes entry (GoRafting, above ~3,500 cfs).

relative_position.notes: Shade right below the left-bend entry and a large diagonal wave tends to flip rafts (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: weaker, but the narrow channel makes it harder to stay off it.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a fully formed raft-flipper guarding the right side; a powerful diagonal wave threatening to flip any boat that goes over it (Garden Betty, run at 1,600 cfs).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger and more powerful with rising water (River Brain); highest flip probability in this band.

relative_position.notes: Precise placement into the gut of the hole surfs the raft next to a big rock on the left below (AW map); clip the main hole on the left side with a slight right angle so it redirects the boat right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: smaller, but the precise-gut placement matters because the surrounding rock is exposed.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a swift-moving hole (Garden Betty); the clip-left, angle-right move is the standard raft line (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: as water levels rise the holes get bigger and more powerful (River Brain).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: River Brain counts two holes - one at the start and one immediately below on the sharp right-hand turn - where other sources describe a single gut hole; disagreement preserved via the separate right-turn hole record.

feature_inventory.display_name: Second hole on the right-hand turn

relative_position.notes: River Brain: a second hole immediately below the first on the sharp right-hand turn; rafts hit both holes while squeezing between Gunsight Rock and the left bank.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: rocky pourover character.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: formed; inflatable kayaks may run the first hole far left and then paddle hard to run right of Gunsight Rock (River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger and more powerful with rising levels (River Brain).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: May be the same feature other sources describe as the right diagonal wave below the left-bend entry; kept separate to preserve the source disagreement.

relative_position.notes: Go too far left and you end up in an eddy that is hard to exit (AW map); advanced boaters eddy left on purpose, then ferry across the hole and exit right of Gunsight Rock (AW map; Garden Betty).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: easy to enter by accident in the narrow channel; the exit ferry is technical.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: kayaks eddied out before ferrying across the currents and gliding past Gunsight Rock (Garden Betty, run at 1,600 cfs).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the eddy line becomes a strong seam that can pull a boat in and spit it back out in seconds (Garden Betty); exiting on line is harder.

relative_position.notes: Left wall below the main hole known as the Peanut Gallery; the clip-left hole move redirects the boat right to avoid it (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: more rock exposed at its base; less push toward the wall.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the main-hole deflection sets the raft away from the wall (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: pushier current drives boats toward the wall if the hole move is missed.

relative_position.notes: The main drop is split in two by Gunsight Rock (OARS); the steep channel runs along it and rafts often end up wrapped (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: maximum exposure; the channel along it is narrowest and wrap risk is highest (AW map low-flow narrow-path note).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: rafts bounce off either side of Gunsight and occasionally go directly over the top (OARS).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more water over and around the rock; bounce-overs are more common, wraps less frequent but higher-energy.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: GoRafting describes a large pyramid rock named Snaggle Tooth marking a drop to its left and never uses the name Gunsight; recorded as a source-name disagreement for the bottom-drop rock.

relative_position.notes: Gunsight Rock forms the final steep drop (AW reach); a falls following the boulder garden (American River Resort); the big drop to the left of Gunsight is the one to avoid (Garden Betty).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: steepest and most consequential; the kayak line charges the drop head-on, aligned between two big boulders (Garden Betty).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a clean drop between the two channels split by Gunsight (OARS; Garden Betty ran it at 1,600 cfs).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the drop smooths into a bigger hydraulic; power over precision.

relative_position.notes: Alternate right line, steep and turbulent with a high likelihood of hitting the right wall (AW reach); hit a large steep wave on the right side (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: not available; insufficient water on the right.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: marginal; the main line remains standard.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: approaching the documented threshold - GoRafting lists Double Trouble as a high-water option above ~3,500 cfs; the AW reach calls it available at higher flows.

relative_position.notes: Above ~3,500 cfs, enter left of the first island by punching two small stacked holes (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: dry or unrunnable.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: not in play; use the right channel of the island.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: close to forming; documented as an option above ~3,500 cfs (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: A rock pile that is a known sieve sits in the center of the flow at the runout; swimmers should escape to river left (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed; swimmers must exit left immediately.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: covered but straining current; the swimmer escape route is river left (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: submerged with a strong draw; keep all swimmers left of center.

relative_position.notes: A medium-size eddy on river right below the final drop; catch it to set safety (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small but easy to catch.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard safety-set eddy for raft trains (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: faster eddy line; still the designated safety and recovery station upstream of the runout sieve.

relative_position.notes: About 0.07 mi below Troublemaker (mile ~5.18), a group of boulders with a small hump and pourover over a submerged boulder containing a foot-entrapment crack (AW reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: hump and pourover most defined; the entrapment crack is shallowest and most dangerous to standing swimmers.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: pourover active; American Whitewater documents fatal and non-fatal entrapments here.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: washed out at the surface, but the crack remains; swimmers from Troublemaker must be recovered before this point.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Documented fatal and non-fatal foot entrapments (AW reach); treat as a swimmer-recovery deadline for the rapid.

class_reported_range.note: The mile guide lists III- at the Gorge entrance; the AW map, AW Gorge reach, GoRafting, and Whitewater Guidebook rate III; River Brain reports III to III+.

relative_position.notes: Two line choices at the entrance at normal summer release flows (GoRafting): hug tight to the left side of House Rock, threading the needle between the center hole and the rock, or start left, avoid the left hole, and ferry right; the river bends left around a gravel bar and runs along the left cliff (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: thread-the-needle tolerances are tightest; the boulder garden is most exposed.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: both documented entry options work (GoRafting normal summer release flows).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: above ~2,500 cfs the left-of-Fowler's-Rock line opens and becomes the line of choice (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: A submerged boulder lies along the house-sized rock on the right; avoid it with a quick left slalom (AW map); GoRafting names House Rock as the entry landmark.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the submerged boulder is barely covered; parking is easiest and hardest to free.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: rafts are often accidentally parked on the submerged boulder (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: deeper cover, but the current onto the boulder is stronger.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Rafts are often accidentally parked here (AW map); a loaded raft parked broadside risks a wrap.

relative_position.notes: A hole in the middle channel between the two entry lines (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a rocky pourover; the needle between it and House Rock is narrowest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the formed hole that the House Rock line threads past (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger, but the opening left line bypasses it entirely (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: A hole on the left side, avoided by the start-left-then-ferry-right line (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: shallow and rocky.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: formed; the ferry-right line steers around it (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: stronger, sitting beside the newly opened high-flow left line.

relative_position.notes: The tall cabin-sized boulder itself; the standard route passes to its right (AW map); the river zig-zags left then right past several very large rocks (River Brain), with smaller boulders creating plenty of hydraulics (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the slalom past the giant rocks is tightest; contact risk is highest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard right-of-rock line (AW map); the first class III below Troublemaker (Whitewater Guidebook).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: above ~2,500 cfs the preferred line moves left of the rock (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Below Fowler's Rock pick a good slot near center through the diagonal boulder fence (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the fence is most exposed and the slot narrow.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the near-center slot is the standard runout (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the fence washes; just below, Son of Fowler builds two holes (top right and mid-river) above ~3,000 cfs - run left to right to bypass them (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Above ~2,500 cfs a line to the left of Fowler's Rock opens up and becomes the line of choice as flows rise (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: not available; insufficient water left of the rock.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: marginal; the two standard entry lines remain preferred (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: open and documented as the line of choice above ~2,500 cfs (GoRafting).

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates II; the AW map and AW Gorge reach II+; River Brain (as Haystack Rapids) II+ to III-; the mile guide lists III.

relative_position.notes: Big standing waves run a long distance through a narrow section of gorge (AW map; AW Gorge reach); a long straight read-and-run section (River Brain); hit the tall waves head-on (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small haystacks; a low-consequence canyon that is still fun (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: classic standing waves - not particularly difficult and a lot of fun (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger waves and more splash; still a low-consequence corridor.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Documented coverage is thin: every consulted source describes only the wave train and the narrow canyon, with no named sub-features.

relative_position.notes: The canyon ends at a calm pool before Lost Hat (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a long, calm regroup pool.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard regroup before the Lost Hat / Satan's Cesspool sequence (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: shorter and faster, but still the last easy recovery before Lost Hat.

class_reported_range.note: The AW map lists III-; GoRafting rates III; River Brain reports III- to III.

relative_position.notes: After a flat section the river drops steeply into big standing waves (AW map); the drop builds nice waves (River Brain); a fun wave train (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a defined drop with modest waves.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard big-standing-wave entry (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: steeper, faster waves feeding directly downstream.

relative_position.notes: The rapid continues unabated into the one below (AW map); it leads directly into Satan's Cesspool - pay attention here (GoRafting; River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: slightly more definition between the two rapids, but recovery room is still minimal.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: continuous; a swimmer at the bottom of Lost Hat reaches Satan's Cesspool without a break (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the sequence effectively merges; at high flows even Satan's and Son of Satan's run together (AW Gorge reach).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: A swimmer or out-of-position raft at the bottom of Lost Hat enters Satan's Cesspool without a break (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Stay right at the bottom if planning to sneak Satan's Cesspool down its right-side slot; otherwise work left for the standard line (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the sneak setup matters most; the right slot below is at its rockiest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: choose the side here before committing (AW map, which questions whether the sneak is necessary).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: harder to move late; commit to a side early in the wave train.

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates III; the mile guide, AW map, AW Gorge reach, Whitewater Guidebook, OARS, and River Brain III+.

relative_position.notes: A long moderate rapid bends left toward a low rocky island (AW Gorge reach); the island splits the right-side sneak from the standard left line.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the island is well exposed and the split is obvious.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard split - sneak right of the island or run the main line left of it (AW Gorge reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: still defined; GoRafting documents the island submerging entirely only above ~6,000 cfs.

relative_position.notes: A narrow rocky slot is available to the right of the central island (AW map); the AW Gorge reach lists the sneak route down the right; set it up by staying right through Lost Hat.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: bony but the documented sneak; the AW map questions whether sneaking is necessary.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: available for crews avoiding the main hit (AW Gorge reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: less distinct as water spreads over the island shoulder.

relative_position.notes: The main current sweeps quickly left at the top of the island, then is forced sharply back right by a cliff wall on the left (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a modest push; the dogleg reads clearly.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the defined dogleg move of the standard line; drive off the wall (AW Gorge reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: strengthening toward GoRafting's ~4,000 cfs mark, where the water pushes hard toward the left wall and the rapid gets a bit flippy.

relative_position.notes: A rock shelf approaches from river right and constricts the channel, throwing a substantial lateral; T it up and be ready for it to turn the boat slightly right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the shelf shows; the lateral is small but the constriction is narrow.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the fully formed lateral to square up (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: a bigger lateral with more deflection toward the left wall.

relative_position.notes: A ledge at top right and immediately another on the left create a crazy, twisting, turbulent drop (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the ledges are most distinct and the twisting drop most technical.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the crux move of the standard line (AW Gorge reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the two ledges smooth into one large hydraulic.

relative_position.notes: An underwater rock cluster forms a hole and a large curling wave at low and moderate flows (AW map); the best candidate for the crux of the Gorge - a large hole as the river cuts right through bedrock (River Brain); the final big drop and standing wave (OARS).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the hole and curling wave are defined at low flows (AW map).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: one of the bigger hits in a raft (AW map); the biggest rapid of the Gorge run (Whitewater Guidebook).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: waves keep building; GoRafting documents them diminishing again only above ~6,000 cfs as the island submerges.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: The signature photo hit of the Gorge; a swimmer released here must reach the left eddy before Son of Satan's (AW map).

relative_position.notes: A second straightforward wave follows the lateral, with swirly water below the waves in the constriction (GoRafting); below the ledges the rapid is clean and deep (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a small follow-up wave over deep water.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard second hit and boily runout (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: stronger boils and swirls in the constriction.

relative_position.notes: Swimmers should enter the left eddy below to avoid getting bruised in Son of Satan just ahead (AW map); a large catch pool below the ledges (AW Gorge reach); a backboard is tied to the left shore below the rapid (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a well-defined pool with easy recovery.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard recovery target - make it before Son of Satan's, only ~70 yards downstream (AW Gorge reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the recovery window shrinks; at high flows Satan's and Son of Satan's run together (AW Gorge reach).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: The swimmer-recovery deadline for the rapid; AW Gorge reach notes the two rapids merge at high flows and rescue gear (backboard) is staged on the left shore (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: Above ~6,000 cfs the waves diminish but a tumultuous eddy line forms along river right below the rapid (GoRafting); photographers commonly shoot from the left cliff top and downstream right (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: absent; the right side below is calm.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: benign; ordinary eddy lines only.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: strengthening seam on the right; the documented tumultuous eddy line fully forms only above ~6,000 cfs (GoRafting).

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates II; the mile guide and AW Gorge reach II+; River Brain II+ to III- (as Dead Man's Drop); the AW map III.

relative_position.notes: A submerged rock sits mid-channel at the top (AW map; GoRafting's covered mid-river rock); the best route in a raft or as a swimmer is just to its right (AW map).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: shallowest cover and the sharpest hit for anything left of the line.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: covered; read the line off the pillow and pass just right (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: washing out, but the left side of the rock remains the bad swim.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: AW map: a swim to the left of the submerged rock will probably kick your butt good.

relative_position.notes: The current heads toward the right wall and piles up on the right bank (AW Gorge reach; River Brain); pass right of the rock without smashing into the right-hand wall (AW map); work left at the bottom (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a mild push with rock showing at the wall base.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard move - there is plenty of room to move left, an easy move even at the last second (AW Gorge reach).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: a stronger pile on the right bank; start the work-left earlier.

relative_position.notes: Enter right of the covered mid-river rock, then work left to finish (GoRafting); a short pool separates the rapid from Satan's Cesspool above (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a precise slot between the rock and the wall.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard right-of-rock, work-left line (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: at high flows Satan's Cesspool and Son of Satan's run together (AW Gorge reach); arrive already set right-of-rock.

relative_position.notes: After a brief calm stretch the river charges over diagonal bedrock ledges along the left wall (AW map); the river bends left above the drop and narrows over the rapid's length (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the ledges are most exposed and read as distinct diagonal drops.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: read and run at the top (GoRafting); the diagonal laterals define the line.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the ledge laterals strengthen as the channel narrows.

relative_position.notes: The rapid finishes around a cabin-sized rock on the right (AW map); pass left of the massive boulder at the bottom (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: maximum exposure; the pass-left channel is narrowest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard pass-left move (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more water piles onto the rock; the move left needs to come earlier.

relative_position.notes: Keep the raft pointed straight through unpredictable water in the narrowing channel (GoRafting); a read-and-run class III as the river turns left toward Lower Haystack Canyon (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: rocky but readable; the squirrelly water is mild.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: keep it straight through the boils between the ledges and the boulder (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: stronger boils and surging water in the constriction.

class_reported_range.note: The AW map, AW Gorge reach, GoRafting, and the mile guide list II+; River Brain reports II+ to III.

relative_position.notes: An extended wave train opens the rapid (GoRafting); big standing waves in a narrow gorge section, a bit more difficult than the upper canyon and a lot of wetness (AW map); mostly continuous waves (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: modest haystacks; a straightforward splashy corridor.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the classic wet ride - a bit more difficult than Upper Haystack (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger continuous waves through the narrow section.

relative_position.notes: A shallow mid-river rock creates a left-side hole and a right-side wave train at normal flows (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the rock is near the surface and the left hole reads as a pourover.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: hole on the left of the rock, wave train on its right (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: building toward GoRafting's 4,500+ cfs threshold where it becomes a substantial crashing wave.

relative_position.notes: Take the right-side wave train past the mid-river rock; even the 4,500+ cfs crashing wave can still be avoided on the right (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: an easy dodge; plenty of definition around the rock.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard line past the rock (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: still open; documented as the avoidance even above 4,500 cfs (GoRafting).

relative_position.notes: A pool follows downstream (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a calm regroup pool.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard recovery before the Bouncing Rock sequence (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: shorter but still a usable regroup.

class_reported_range.note: The mile guide and OARS list II+; the AW map, AW Gorge reach, GoRafting, and Whitewater Guidebook III; River Brain II+ to III.

relative_position.notes: Most of the current smashes into a rock on the right at the apex of an outside bend (AW map; GoRafting); guides often deliberately bounce the raft off it for giggles (AW map), using the current to sling into the big hole (OARS).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the rock is prominent and the bounce is a firm hit.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard bounce move - the current does the work (OARS).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: a strong sweep onto the rock; AW Gorge reach warns crews may not have the paddling skill to avoid being swept in.

relative_position.notes: Below the rock the current pours into a large flippy hole (AW map); the river drops into a fun hole with a slight lateral shape at the bend apex - hit it head-on (GoRafting); a steep drop over the bouncing rock and into the hole (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: smaller and rockier; still a defined drop.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: fully formed and flippy if taken sideways off the rock (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the biggest hit; the lateral shape strengthens and punishes angled entries.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: The AW map calls the hole large and flippy; the deliberate bounce line feeds it at an angle.

relative_position.notes: Sneak options run down the left (River Brain); there is plenty of room to avoid the rock and hole, but crews may lack the paddling skill to avoid being swept in (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: easy to reach; the inside of the bend is shallow but open.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the documented avoidance for crews skipping the bounce (River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the outside-bend sweep makes the move harder to hold (AW Gorge reach).

feature_inventory.display_name: Exit wave train at Weber Creek

relative_position.notes: The rapid finishes with a pleasant wave train (GoRafting); Weber Creek enters from river left during the rapid (Whitewater Guidebook).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a mellow runout.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard closing wave train (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: larger waves; Weber Creek inflow adds volume in wet seasons.

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates II; the AW map III-; River Brain (as Triage) III- to III; the AW Gorge reach III.

feature_inventory.display_name: Standing waves on the right bend

relative_position.notes: Big standing waves as the river bends steadily right toward Hospital Bar, running mostly north (AW map; AW Gorge reach); more waves and possible holes as the river continues to turn (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: modest waves with more exposed rock along the banks.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard read-and-run wave train (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bigger waves; GoRafting documents an enormous hole forming at elevated flows.

relative_position.notes: Rocks and holes line the banks on both sides with the clean water in the middle (AW map); recorded on the left with the hazard mirrored on the right bank.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the bank rocks are most exposed; stay off the edges.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: holes pocket both banks (AW map); the center stays clean.
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: bank holes strengthen and edge eddies get grabby.

relative_position.notes: Right of center, read and run (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: simple read-and-run.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard right-of-center line (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: watch for the enormous hole GoRafting documents at elevated flows as the wave train grows.

class_reported_range.note: The mile guide, AW map, GoRafting, Whitewater Guidebook, and OARS rate III; the AW Gorge reach and River Brain report up to III+.

relative_position.notes: A small boulder garden leads in; most of the current then drops right over a boulder bar (AW map); the river bends right past boulders (AW Gorge reach); a rocky section leads into the main hole (River Brain).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the boulder bar is most exposed; the drop right is a defined move.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard rocky lead-in over the bar (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the bar covers and the entry becomes a fast tongue into the chute.

relative_position.notes: The current funnels into a fast channel along the right bank (AW map); the river sweeps left with the right side funneling into the lateral wave (GoRafting); then left through a chute (AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a narrow but readable channel along the right.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard approach that delivers the raft onto the diagonal hole (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: faster and pushier; commit to an angle before the chute.

relative_position.notes: A left-facing diagonal hole/lateral wave at the bottom; the farther right you are on the hole the bigger and more retentive it gets (River Brain); T up square to the lateral for the biggest hit (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: a smaller, rockier hit; still the wettest moment of the Gorge run.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the last big ride (Whitewater Guidebook); a big fun wet hit (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: the largest wave on the river (AW Gorge reach); the right side grows increasingly retentive (River Brain).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Named for a hospital tent set up during the Gold Rush (AW map; OARS); running right offers photographer opportunities with flip potential (AW map).

relative_position.notes: Shallow rocks along the left bank known as the Catchers Mitt can snag rafts; the photographer sits just above them - smile if you hang up (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: most exposed; the snag zone is widest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the documented snag hazard bordering the drier left line (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: partially covered but still shallow enough to catch a raft drifting left.

relative_position.notes: The diagonal hole runs best on the left (AW map); left routing provides drier passage (GoRafting), threading between the hole and the Catchers Mitt rocks.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: narrow between the exposed Catchers Mitt and the hole.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard drier line for crews avoiding the big hit (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more usable as the Mitt covers, but the hole's left shoulder also grows.

class_reported_range.note: The AW map lists III- falling to class II at most when Folsom Reservoir is full; GoRafting II; the mile guide II+; River Brain III- to III.

relative_position.notes: A sneaker hole sits top-center; GoRafting names it the Little Big Hole.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small but distinct and grabby for its size.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the documented sneaker hole - easy to clip unaware (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: washes toward a wave, but the surge remains.

relative_position.notes: A pleasant wave train forms center-left (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: small waves; the rapid reads as a riffle when the reservoir is high.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard center-left ride (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: larger waves feeding the bottom hole when it is exposed.

relative_position.notes: When Folsom Reservoir is down, the hole at the bottom is a miniature version of Hospital Bar; when the reservoir is full the rapid is class II at most (AW map); paddle and punch through it (River Brain); at high reservoir the hole washes out (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: reservoir stage matters more than release level here; with the lake down the mini-Hospital-Bar hole is at its rockiest.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: with the reservoir down, punch the bottom hole (River Brain); with the lake full the rapid fades to class II at most (AW map).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more push through the hole when exposed; reservoir stage still decides whether it exists at all (GoRafting).

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Reservoir stage is an independent variable from release cfs for this rapid and must be authored separately in review scenarios.

class_reported_range.note: GoRafting rates II; the mile guide III; the AW map II-III depending on season; River Brain III- to III, present only in low-reservoir years.

relative_position.notes: At a wide gravel bar the river bends left into a fast chute (AW map); a funnel constriction as the river bends left (River Brain); the rapid uncovers late in summer as Folsom Reservoir drops and can be submerged entirely when the lake is high (AW map; GoRafting; AW Gorge reach).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: with the lake low the chute is fast and defined; late-season boaters get surprised as it uncovers (AW map).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: the standard funnel when exposed; the rapid only exists in low-reservoir conditions (River Brain).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: a pushier chute when exposed; reservoir stage remains the controlling variable.

feature_inventory.consequence_notes: Reservoir stage is an independent variable from release cfs for this rapid and must be authored separately in review scenarios.

relative_position.notes: A big center rock forms a pourover and hole in the chute - run over it into the hole if you're brave (AW map); a large, surprising wave/hole (AW Gorge reach); a mid-river pourover rock in the wave train (GoRafting).

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: the rock is shallowest and the pourover most abrupt; the late-summer surprise at its sharpest (AW map).
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: a formed pourover/hole in the wave train when exposed (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: more water over the rock; the hole becomes a large crashing wave/hole (AW Gorge reach).

relative_position.notes: The AW map sneaks the rock on the right; GoRafting routes left to avoid it; the AW Gorge reach says the wave/hole can be avoided on either side - side-preference disagreement preserved.

flow_dependence.low_review: 900 cfs: both edges are shallow; pick the side with visible water.
flow_dependence.reference_review: 1,600 cfs: either side works (AW Gorge reach); the sources split on right (AW map) versus left (GoRafting).
flow_dependence.high_review: 3,000 cfs: wider margins on both sides as the chute fills.
